_about: Declares this repo's cross-agent context layout for tools/check-agent-context.mjs (canon §6/§7). Each fork edits this to point at its own root rules file and adapters.
_agentFileAllowlistNote: Repo-wide paths whose basename matches agentFilePattern but which are NOT agent-rules constitutions (templates, examples, integration docs). Empty after the 2026-05-23 legacy reap removed the two former entries (a generated v1 project-constitution and Gemini integration docs). Check #6 scans the whole repo (finding #2): the root authority (rootRulesFile) + declared adapters are auto-legit; everything else matching the pattern must be allowlisted here or it is flagged as a parallel constitution.
_brandExclusionNote: L1 neutrality fire-test (canon §8 / finding F1): the neutralL1Files below must name no product/brand. Word-boundary + case-insensitive. Org/methodology names (VibeThink, VT-Method) may appear in L2/portal docs (e.g. START-HERE, VT-METHOD), never in these neutral cores.
_agentConfigsNote: Empty on purpose (criterion #8): the root authority is under the strictest budget (32 KiB) natively, so no per-agent override is required for this kit. A fork whose root exceeds a strict agent's budget MUST declare its tracked override file here (e.g. .codex/config.toml).
_ruleNumberPatternNote: null = this root uses LEVEL sections, not a numbered rule list, so the sequence check is skipped. A repo with numbered rules sets this to e.g. "^> ?(\\d+)\\." to enable the gap/duplicate check.
_secretPatternsNote: Heuristic, high-signal patterns only — this is a BASIC pattern scan (finding #5), NOT a full secret scanner. It catches known provider key shapes; it does not prove the absence of secrets. Covers the providers this ecosystem actually uses (GitHub, OpenAI, Slack, AWS, Google/Gemini, Anthropic, Stripe) + PEM private keys.
